page: Amazon Web Services c3.xlarge is a legacy compute-optimized instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 4 dedicated virtual CPUs across 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, the processor includes a 25 MB L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX) and AES hardware-accelerated cryptography. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.88 GB per core, and includes 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the c3.xlarge exhibits performance characteristics typical of older hardware generations. In stress-ng testing, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier, with scores of 1316.08 and 3460.56 respectively. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with large-block read bandwidth at 26.21 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 22.34 GB/sec, both ranking in the bottom 25%. Geekbench 6 testing reflects these limitations, yielding a single-core score of 685.0 and a multi-core score of 1486.0, placing the instance in the bottom 10% of modern cloud hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3.xlarge offers a dedicated hardware profile with bundled local NVMe SSD storage, but its resource density and processing throughput are low compared to modern compute-optimized instances. The instance is best suited for legacy workloads, basic web serving, or lightweight batch processing that requires dedicated vCPUs and local scratch space but does not demand high computational throughput or memory capacity. It is not recommended for modern database operations, large-scale LLM inference, or intensive multi-threaded applications due to its poor performance in database and cryptographic benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services c3.xlarge is a legacy compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 2.8 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Xen hypervisor, it includes 8.0 GB of RAM, 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 1 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark data places this instance in the lower tiers of modern cloud performance, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% categories. Large-block memory read bandwidth is limited to 26.21 GB/sec. Given these performance constraints and its older hardware profile, the c3.xlarge is best suited for legacy applications, basic web serving, and lightweight batch processing tasks that benefit from dedicated vCPUs and local SSD storage but do not require high computational or memory throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c3.xlarge is a legacy compute-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 80 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering basic performance for legacy workloads and lightweight web serving.

tagline: A legacy compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated vCPUs and local NVMe SSD storage for basic workloads and lightweight web serving.

8.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Xen hypervisor architecture with 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Performance benchmarks fall primarily within the bottom 10% to 25% of cloud servers
Suitable for legacy applications, basic web serving, and lightweight batch processing
